title: simplicio.remote-worker/v2
description: Immutable task envelope published by a Producer/Coordinator into the simplicio.queue/v1 store and pulled/claimed by an independent RemoteWorkerDaemon on a different device/process (issue #286). This is the wire contract for the task payload carried inside RemoteQueue.enqueue()/task()['payload']; it is deliberately separate from the queue's own lease/fencing envelope (simplicio.queue/v1, simplicio_loop/remote_queue.py), which owns task_id/attempt_id/lease_id/agent_id/fencing_token and is never duplicated here.

task_id.description: Canonical task identifier; matches the simplicio.queue/v1 task_id this envelope is enqueued under.

created_at.description: Coordinator-assigned creation timestamp (server clock authority remains the queue, not this field).

idempotency_key.description: Enqueue-side idempotency key; re-publishing the same task_id with the same key must be a no-op.

depends_on.description: Other task_ids in the same queue that must be completed before this task is pull-eligible.

base_sha.description: Exact commit the worker must materialize its workspace from; a worker MUST refuse to start if this SHA does not exist or diverges after fetch.

allowed_paths.description: Path/glob allow-list; the worker's workspace write access is limited to these entries.

capabilities_required.description: Subset check against the claiming worker's registered capabilities (simplicio_loop.agent_contract identity).

context_digest.description: SHA-256 of the canonical (sort_keys=True) JSON serialization of the fields context-relevant to execution (goal, acceptance_criteria, source, allowed_paths); a worker MUST verify this before creating a workspace and MUST refuse the task on mismatch.
